elements.name: Adverse Event Condition
elements.description: Change to patient condition that could be an unintended effect of clinical interventions. Examples include but are not limited to rash, fracture, fever, etc.

elements.name: Adverse Event Outcome
elements.description: Resulting patient status or consequence following an unexpected condition change or clinical event. Examples include but are not limited to hospitalized, recovered, recovered with sequelae, and death.

elements.name: Drug Class Allergy Intolerance
elements.description: Pharmacologic category for an agent believed to cause a harmful or undesired physiologic response following exposure.

elements.name: Medication Allergy Intolerance
elements.description: Pharmacologic agent believed to cause a harmful or undesired physiologic response following exposure.

elements.name: Non-Medication Allergy Intolerance
elements.description: Non-pharmacologic agent believed to cause a harmful or undesired physiologic response following exposure. Examples include but are not limited to latex, eggs, pollen, and peanuts.

elements.name: Reaction
elements.description: Harmful or undesired physiologic response following exposure to a substance

elements.name: Reaction Date and Time
elements.description: Last date and time a patient had a reaction to an allergen.

elements.name: Allergy Intolerance Onset Date and Time
elements.description: Date and time when an allergy or intolerance developed.

elements.name: Allergy Intolerance Recorded Date and Time
elements.description: Date and time of documentation for the allergy.

elements.name: Assessment and Plan of Treatment
elements.description: Health professional's conclusions and working assumptions that will guide treatment of the patient.

elements.name: Care Plan
elements.description: Shared plan informed by members of a coordinated care team that details conditions, needs, and goals along with strategies for addressing them. Usage notes: Includes problems, health concerns, assessments, goals, and interventions from across care settings. Examples include but are not limited to clinical care plans, condition-specific care plans, coordinated care plan.

elements.name: Care Team Member Identifier
elements.description: Sequence of characters used to uniquely refer to a member of the care team. Examples include but are not limited to National Provider Identifier (NPI) and National Council of State Boards of Nursing Identifier (NCSBN ID).

elements.name: Care Team Member Location
elements.description: Place where care is delivered by a care team member. Examples include but are not limited to clinic address, and location description.

elements.name: Care Team Member Name
elements.description: Word or phrase that constitutes the distinctive designation of a care team member. Examples include but are not limited to given name, middle name, surname, and name suffix.

elements.name: Care Team Member Role
elements.description: Responsibility of an individual within the care team. Examples include but are not limited to primary care physician and caregiver.

elements.name: Care Team Member Telecom
elements.description: Phone or email contact information for a care team member.

elements.name: Consultation Note
elements.description: Narrative summary of care provided in response to a request from a clinician for an opinion, advice, or service. Examples include but are not limited to dermatology, dentistry, and acupuncture.

elements.name: Discharge Summary Note
elements.description: Narrative summary of a patient's admission and course in a hospital or post-acute care setting. Usage note: Must contain admission and discharge dates and locations, discharge instructions, and reason(s) for hospitalization. Examples include but are not limited to dermatology discharge summary, hematology discharge summary, and neurology discharge summary.

elements.name: Emergency Department Note
elements.description: Narrative summary of care delivered in an emergency department.

elements.name: History & Physical
elements.description: Narrative summary of current and past conditions and observations used to inform an episode of care. Examples include but are not limited to admission, surgery, and other procedure.

elements.name: Operative Note
elements.description: Narrative summary of a surgical procedure. Usage note: May include procedures performed, operative and anesthesia times, findings observed, fluids administered, specimens obtained, and complications identified.

elements.name: Procedure Note
elements.description: Narrative summary of non-operative procedure. Examples include but are not limited to interventional cardiology, gastrointestinal endoscopy, and osteopathic manipulation.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core DiagnosticReport Profile for Report and Note Exchange

elements.name: Progress Note
elements.description: Narrative summary of a patient's interval status during an encounter. Examples include but are not limited to hospitalization, outpatient visit, and treatment with a post-acute care provider, or other healthcare encounter.

elements.name: Clinical Test
elements.description: Non-imaging or non-laboratory test. Examples include but are not limited to electrocardiogram (ECG), visual acuity exam, macular exam, and graded exercise testing (GXT).

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Observation Clinical Result
US Quality Core DiagnosticReport Profile for Report and Note Exchange

elements.name: Clinical Test Result/Report
elements.description: Findings of clinical tests.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Observation Clinical Result
US Quality Core DiagnosticReport Profile for Report and Note Exchange

elements.name: Clinical Test Status
elements.description: The completion status of a clinical test at the level of a single observation.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Observation Clinical Result
US Quality Core DiagnosticReport Profile for Report and Note Exchange

elements.name: Communication Status
elements.description: The current state of the communication transition.

elements.name: Communication Topic
elements.description: Purpose or content of communication.

elements.name: Reason Not Communicated
elements.description: The reason why a communication did not happen.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Communication Not Done

elements.name: Diagnostic Imaging Reference
elements.description: The technical details of the retrieval access path to indicate network services that can be used to access DICOM studies, series, or instances content and metadata (e.g., a DICOMweb server endpoint providing QIDO-RS, WADO-RS, or WADO-URI).

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core DiagnosticReport Profile for Report and Note Exchange

elements.name: Diagnostic Imaging Report
elements.description: Interpreted results of imaging tests. Usage Note: Includes both structured and unstructured (narrative) components.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core DiagnosticReport Profile for Report and Note Exchange
US Quality Core Observation Clinical Result

elements.name: Diagnostic Imaging Status
elements.description: The current status of the imaging study- registered, available, cancelled, entered-in-error, or unknown.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core DiagnosticReport Profile for Report and Note Exchange

elements.name: Diagnostic Imaging Test
elements.description: Tests that generate visual images and require interpretation by qualified professionals. Examples include but are not limited to computed tomography-head, radiograph-chest, and ultrasound-pelvis.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core DiagnosticReport Profile for Report and Note Exchange
US Quality Core Observation Clinical Result

elements.name: Diagnosis Rank
elements.description: Ordinal number or code that identifies the priority or significance of a diagnosis for a given encounter.
elements.notes: In QI-Core, information about principal diagnosis is represented using the Claim profile. In US Quality Core v1.0.0-ballot, support for the Claim profile is not required for conformance. We encourage the quality community to provide additional input on information needed to support quality measurement use cases through the HL7 ballot process for US Quality Core.

elements.name: Encounter Diagnosis
elements.description: Coded diagnoses associated with an episode of care.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Condition Encounter Diagnosis

elements.name: Encounter Diagnosis Role
elements.description: The specific use or role of diagnosis within the context of an encounter e.g., admitting diagnosis, principal diagnosis, discharge diagnosis, etc.

elements.name: Encounter Disposition
elements.description: Place or setting where the patient went after a hospital stay or encounter.

elements.name: Encounter Identifier
elements.description: Sequence of characters by which an encounter is known.

elements.name: Encounter Location
elements.description: Place where a patient's care is delivered.

elements.name: Encounter Status
elements.description: Current state of the encounter: Planned; Arrived; Triaged; In progress; On leave; Finished; Canceled; Entered in error; Unknown.

elements.name: Encounter Time
elements.description: Date/times related to an encounter. Examples include but are not limited to scheduled appointment time, check in time, and start and stop times.

elements.name: Encounter Type
elements.description: Category of health care service. Examples include but are not limited to office visit, telephone assessment, and home visit.

elements.name: Present on Admission
elements.description: An indicator of whether a patient's condition or diagnosis was known to be present at the time of admission.
elements.notes: In QI-Core, information about present on admission is represented using the Claim profile. In US Quality Core v1.0.0, support for the Claim profile is not required for conformance. We have reintroduced the Present on Admission extension for Encounter.diagnosis in this US Quality Core v1.0.0-ballot and it is (USCDI+ Quality) tagged.

elements.name: Facility Address
elements.description: Physical location of available services or resources.

elements.name: Facility Identifier
elements.description: Sequence of characters representing a physical place of available services or resources.

elements.name: Facility Name
elements.description: Word or words by which a facility is known.

elements.name: Facility Type
elements.description: Category of service or resource available in a location. Examples include but are not limited to hospital, laboratory, pharmacy, ambulatory clinic, long-term and post-acute care facility, and food pantry.

elements.name: Family Health History
elements.description: Family member health condition(s) that are relevant to a patient's care.

elements.name: Advance Directive Observation
elements.description: Information about a patient or provider authored document indicating its location, content, type, and verification status. Usage note: May include structured or unstructured data, whether a person has one or more advance directive documents, the type of advance directive, the location of the document, and whether it has been verified. Such documents may be used should a person be unable to communicate their wishes, preferences, or priorities to their provider. Examples include but are not limited to an indication that a living will is on file, a reference to the location of durable medical power of attorney, and the validating provider.

usCore.title: US Core Observation ADI Documentation Profile

elements.name: Care Experience Preference
elements.description: Person's goals, preferences, and priorities for overall experiences during their care and treatment. Examples include but are not limited to religious beliefs, dislikes and fears, and thoughts and feelings to be shared.

usCore.title: US Core Treatment Intervention Preference Profile

elements.name: Patient Goals
elements.description: Desired outcomes of patient's care. Examples include but are not limited to blood pressure control, functional ability, and nutrition.

elements.name: SDOH Goals
elements.description: Desired future states for an identified Social Determinants of Health-related health concern, condition, or diagnosis. Examples include but are not limited to food security, transportation security, and ability to access health care.

elements.name: Treatment Intervention Preference
elements.description: Person's goals, preferences, and priorities for care and treatment in case that person is unable to make medical decisions because of a serious illness or injury. Examples include but are not limited to thoughts on cardiopulmonary resuscitation, mental health treatment preferences, and thoughts on pain management.

usCore.title: US Core Treatment Intervention Preference Profile

elements.name: Coverage Status
elements.description: Presence or absence of health care insurance.

elements.name: Coverage Type
elements.description: Category of healthcare payers, insurance products, or benefits. Examples include but are not limited to Medicaid, commercial, HMO, Veterans Benefits Administration, Medicare Part D, and dental.

elements.name: Group Identifier
elements.description: Sequence of characters used to uniquely refer to a specific health insurance plan.

elements.name: Health Insurance Beneficiary
elements.description: The party who benefits from the health insurance coverage.

elements.name: Health Insurance Coverage Period
elements.description: The time frame in which the policy is in force.

elements.name: Health Insurance Payer
elements.description: Issuer of the policy.

elements.name: Health Insurance Plan Identifier
elements.description: Sequence of characters used to uniquely refer to an insurance plan.

elements.name: Insurance Plan Name
elements.description: Name of the health plan benefit offering assigned to the Plan Identifier.

elements.name: Member Identifier
elements.description: Sequence of characters used to uniquely refer to an individual with respect to their insurance.

elements.name: Payer Identifier
elements.description: Sequence of characters used to uniquely refer to an insurance payer.

elements.name: Relationship to Subscriber
elements.description: Relationship of a patient to the primary insured person.

elements.name: Subscriber Identifier
elements.description: Sequence of characters used to uniquely refer to the individual that selects insurance benefits.

elements.name: Alcohol Use
elements.description: Evaluation of a patient's consumption of alcohol. Examples include but are not limited to history of alcohol use, alcohol use disorder identification test and alcohol intake assessment.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Observation Screening Assessment
US Quality Core Condition Problems Health Concerns

elements.name: Disability Status
elements.description: Assessment of a patient's physical, cognitive, or psychiatric disabilities. Examples include but are not limited to American Community Survey, Veterans RAND Health Survey, and Patient-Reported Outcomes Measurement Information System (PROMIS).

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Observation Screening Assessment
US Quality Core Condition Problems Health Concerns

elements.name: Functional Status
elements.description: Assessment of a person's ability to perform activities of daily living and activities across other situations and settings. Examples include but are not limited to Functional Assessment Standardized Items (FASI) and Timed Up and Go (TUG).

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Observation Screening Assessment
US Quality Core Condition Problems Health Concerns

elements.name: Health Concerns
elements.description: Health-related issue or worry. Examples include but are not limited to weight gain and cancer risk.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Condition Problems Health Concerns

elements.name: Mental/Cognitive Status
elements.description: Assessment or screening for the presence of a mental or behavioral problem. Examples include but are not limited to Confusion Assessment Method (CAM) and Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ).

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Observation Screening Assessment
US Quality Core Condition Problems Health Concerns

elements.name: Physical Activity
elements.description: Evaluation of a patient's current or usual exercise. Examples include but are not limited to frequency of muscle-strengthening physical activity, days per week with moderate to strenuous physical activity, and minutes per day of moderate to strenuous physical activity.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Observation Screening Assessment
US Quality Core Condition Problems Health Concerns

elements.name: Pregnancy Status
elements.description: State or condition of being pregnant or intent to become pregnant. Examples include but are not limited to pregnant, not pregnant, and unknown.

usCore.title: US Core Observation Pregnancy Status Profile
US Core Observation Pregnancy Intent Profile

elements.name: SDOH Assessment
elements.description: Screening questionnaire-based, structured evaluation for a Social Determinants of Health-related risk. Examples include but are not limited to food, housing, transportation security, and health literacy.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Observation Screening Assessment
US Quality Core Condition Problems Health Concerns

elements.name: Smoking Status
elements.description: Assessment of a patient's smoking behaviors. Examples include but are not limited to pack- years and current use.

usCore.title: US Core Smoking Status Observation Profile

elements.name: Substance Use
elements.description: Evaluation of a patient's reported use of drugs or other substances for non-medical purposes or in excess of a valid prescription. Examples include but are not limited to substance use disorder score, and substance use knowledge assessment.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Observation Screening Assessment
US Quality Core Condition Problems Health Concerns

elements.name: Reason Not Performed
elements.description: Explanation or justification provided when an order or practice guideline is not carried out. Usage note: Should be included with a procedure, immunization, and medication.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Procedure Not Done
US Quality Core Immunization Not Done
US Quality Core MedicationAdministration Not Done

elements.name: Immunization Record Source
elements.description: Immunization event information source. Examples include but are not limited to facility administering the immunization and an external record.

elements.name: Immunization Status
elements.description: State of an immunization event.

elements.name: Immunizations
elements.description: Vaccine product administered, planned, or reported.

elements.name: Lot Number
elements.description: Sequence of characters representing a specific quantity of manufactured material within a batch of a vaccine product.

elements.name: Vaccination Administration Date and Time
elements.description: The date and time the vaccination event occurred.

elements.name: Result Interpretation
elements.description: Categorical assessment of a laboratory value, often in relation to a test's reference range. Examples include but are not limited to high, low, critical high, and normal.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Laboratory Result Observation

elements.name: Result Reference Range
elements.description: Upper and lower limit of quantitative test values expected for a designated population of individuals.Usage note: Reference range values may differ by patient characteristics, laboratory test manufacturer, and laboratory test performer.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Laboratory Result Observation

elements.name: Result Status
elements.description: State or condition of a laboratory test.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Laboratory Result Observation
US Quality Core DiagnosticReport Profile for Laboratory Results Reporting

elements.name: Result Unit of Measure
elements.description: Unit of measurement to report quantitative results.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Laboratory Result Observation

elements.name: Specimen Condition Acceptability
elements.description: Information about a specimen, including the container, that is used to determine a laboratory's criteria for acceptability. Usage note: This may include information about the contents of the container, the container, and the label. Examples include but are not limited to hemolyzed, clotted, container leaking, and missing patient name.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Laboratory Result Observation

elements.name: Specimen Identifier
elements.description: Sequence of characters assigned by a laboratory for an individual specimen. Example includes but is not limited to accession number.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Laboratory Result Observation

elements.name: Specimen Source Site
elements.description: Body location from where a specimen was obtained. Examples include but are not limited to right internal jugular, left arm, and right eye.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Laboratory Result Observation

elements.name: Specimen Type
elements.description: Substance being sampled or tested. Examples include but are not limited to nasopharyngeal swab, whole blood, serum, urine, and wound swab.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Laboratory Result Observation

elements.name: Tests
elements.description: Analysis of specimens derived from humans which provide information for the diagnosis, prevention,treatmentofdisease,orassessment of health.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Laboratory Result Observation
US Quality Core DiagnosticReport Profile for Laboratory Results Reporting

elements.name: Values/Results
elements.description: Documentedfindingsofatestedspecimen including structured and unstructured components

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Laboratory Result Observation
US Quality Core DiagnosticReport Profile for Laboratory Results Reporting

elements.name: Laboratory Result Report Date and Time
elements.description: The date and time when the laboratory result(s) report is made available to inform clinical decision making.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Laboratory Result Observation

elements.name: Specimen Collection Date and Time
elements.description: Date and time the test sample was collected.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Laboratory Result Observation

elements.name: Device Type
elements.description: Kind of instrument, machine, appliance, implant, software, and similar medical device.

elements.name: Unique Device Identifier (UDI)
elements.description: Numeric or alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies a medical device. Usage note: Contains a device identifier (DI) and one or more production identifiers (PI).

elements.name: Days Supply
elements.description: Number of days supply of medication dispensed by the pharmacy.

elements.name: Dispense Status
elements.description: State of a medication with regards to dispensing or other activity. Examples include but are not limited to dispensed, partially dispensed, not dispensed.

elements.name: Dose
elements.description: Amount of a medication for each administration.

elements.name: Dose Unit of Measure
elements.description: Units of measure of a medication. Examples include but are not limited to milligram (mg) and milliliter (mL).

elements.name: Indication
elements.description: Sign, symptom, or medical condition that is the reason for giving or taking a medication.

elements.name: Medication Adherence
elements.description: Statement of whether a medication has been consumed according to instructions. Examples include but are not limited to taking as directed, taking less than directed, and not taking.

elements.name: Medication Administration
elements.description: Information about the event of a patient consuming or otherwise being given a medication. Examples include but are not limited to swallowing a tablet, administering an injection, and a long running infusion.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core MedicationAdministration Not Done

elements.name: Medication Instructions
elements.description: Directions for administering or taking a medication. Usage note: May include route, quantity, timing/frequency, and special instructions (PRN, sliding scale, taper). Examples include but are not limited to prescription directions for taking a medication, and package instructions for over-the-counter medications.

elements.name: Medication Quantity
elements.description: The amount of medication, including the unit of measure, that is prescribed or dispensed.

elements.name: Medications
elements.description: Pharmacologicagentusedinthediagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease.

elements.name: Route of Administration
elements.description: Physiological administration path of a therapeutic agent into or onto a patient. Examples include but are not limited to oral, topical, and intravenous.

elements.name: Date and Time Medication Administered
elements.description: A specific date/time or interval of time during which the administration took place (or did not take place, when the 'notGiven' attribute is true).

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core MedicationAdministration Not Done

elements.name: Date and Time Medication Prescribed
elements.description: The date when the prescription was initially written or authored.

elements.name: Birth Outcome
elements.description: The clinical status and health indicators of the newborn including clinical event during labor, delivery, and the immediate post-partum period.

elements.name: Birth Weight
elements.description: The weight of the infant/fetus at birth/delivery.

elements.name: Gestational Age at Delivery
elements.description: Gestational age at delivery refers to how far along the pregnancy is at delivery, generally expressed as a combination of weeks and days.

elements.name: Birth Date and Time
elements.description: The recorded date time of birth of the newborn documented in the fetal record.

elements.name: Clinical Test Order
elements.description: Provider-authored request for the performance of a non-laboratory or non-imaging test.

elements.name: Diagnostic Imaging Order
elements.description: Provider-authored request for the performance of a diagnostic imaging study.

elements.name: Laboratory Order
elements.description: Provider-authored request for laboratory testing.

elements.name: Medical Device Order
elements.description: Provider-authored request for medical devices. Examples include but are not limited to therapeutic footwear, insulin infusion pump, and continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) machine.

elements.name: Medication Order
elements.description: Provider-authored request for the dispensing of a therapeutic agent.

elements.name: Nutrition Order
elements.description: A provider-authored request for nutrition support such as therapeutic diets, enteral feedings, parenteral nutrition, or oral nutritional supplements.

elements.name: Portable Medical Order
elements.description: Provider-authored request for end-of-life or life-sustaining care for a person who has a serious life-limiting medical condition. Usage note: These are meant to follow a person regardless of when and where such an order might be needed (e.g., hospital, care facility, community, home). There are variations in requirements and names for portable medical orders based on jurisdiction. Examples include, but are not limited to, POLST (Portable Medical Order for Life-Sustaining Treatment), MOLST (Medical Orders for Life-Sustaining Treatment), and out-of-hospital DNR (do-not-resuscitate).

elements.name: Procedure Order
elements.description: Provider-authored request for the delivery of patient care services.

elements.name: Current Address
elements.description: Place where a person is located or may be contacted.

elements.name: Date of Birth
elements.description: Known or estimated year, month, and day of the patient's birth.

elements.name: Date of Death
elements.description: Known or estimated year, month, and day of the patient's death.

elements.name: Email Address
elements.description: Unique identifier of an individual's email account that is used to send and receive email messages.

elements.name: Ethnicity
elements.description: Patient's self-identification as Hispanic/ Latino or Non- Hispanic/ Non-Latino.

elements.name: First Name
elements.description: A personal name used before a family name.

elements.name: Interpreter Needed
elements.description: Indication of whether a person needs language interpretation services.

elements.name: Last Name
elements.description: Portion of one's personal name indicating familial or communal ties.

elements.name: Middle Name (Including middle initial)
elements.description: A second or subsequent given name, between the first given name and the family name or surname.

elements.name: Name Suffix
elements.description: Name component following family name that may be used to describe a person's position in a family.

elements.name: Occupation
elements.description: Type of work of a person. Examples include but are not limited to infantry, business analyst, and social worker.

elements.name: Occupation Industry
elements.description: Type of business that compensates for work or assigns work to an unpaid worker or volunteer. Examples include but are not limited to U.S. Army, cement manufacturing, and children and youth services.

elements.name: Patient Identifier
elements.description: An identifier for the patient.

elements.name: Phone Number
elements.description: Numbers and symbols to contact an individual when using a phone.

elements.name: Phone Number Type
elements.description: Contact point when using a phone. Examples include but are not limited to home, work, and mobile.

elements.name: Preferred Language
elements.description: Documented language that the patient would like to use when discussing health care.

elements.name: Previous Address
elements.description: Prior place where a person may have been located or could have been contacted. Includes street name, number, city/town, state, and zip code.

elements.name: Previous Name
elements.description: Any first and last name an individual may have used in the past for any reason.

elements.name: Race
elements.description: An individual's response to the race question based upon self-identification.

elements.name: Related Person's Name
elements.description: Name of a person with a legal or familial relationship to a patient.

elements.name: Relationship Type
elements.description: Relationship of a person to a patient. Examples include but are not limited to parent, next-of-kin, guardian, and custodian.

elements.name: Sex
elements.description: Documentation of a specific instance of sex.

elements.name: Tribal Affiliation
elements.description: Tribe or band with which an individual associates.

elements.name: Delivery Procedures
elements.description: The method by which a baby is delivered (e.g., vaginal delivery, cesarean section).

elements.name: Estimated Date of Delivery
elements.description: The estimated due date (EDD) is the date that spontaneous onset of labor is expected to occur.

elements.name: Fetal Presentation
elements.description: The anatomical part of the fetus closest to the maternal pelvic inlet at the onset of labor.

elements.name: Gestational Age
elements.description: The estimated age of the pregnancy, expressed in weeks and fractions of weeks.

elements.name: Gravidity
elements.description: The number of pregnancies, current and past, regardless of the pregnancy outcome.

elements.name: Parity
elements.description: Parity is defined as the number of pregnancies reaching 20 weeks and 0 days ofgestation or beyond, including the present pregnancy

elements.name: Pregnancy Outcomes
elements.description: The clinical conclusion of the pregnancy focusing on the end status of the pregnancy and the clinical status of the mother.

elements.name: Preterm Births
elements.description: Number of births or deliveries that have occurred before 37 0/7 weeks of gestation

elements.name: Term Births
elements.description: Number of deliveries that have occurred at 37 0/7 weeks of gestation, or after.

elements.name: Delivery Date and Time
elements.description: The date and time when the newborn is completely delivered, either by full expulsion from the birth canal or full removal from the uterus, documented in the maternal record.

elements.name: Condition Status
elements.description: Statement of how a diagnosis, problem, or condition presents or manifests in the patient. Examples include but are not limited to active, resolved, recurrence, and remission.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Condition Problems Health Concerns

elements.name: Date of Diagnosis
elements.description: Date of first determination by a qualified professional of the presence of a problem or condition affecting a patient.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Condition Problems Health Concerns
US Quality Core Condition Encounter Diagnosis

elements.name: Date of Onset
elements.description: Date when a problem/condition started.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Condition Problems Health Concerns
US Quality Core Condition Encounter Diagnosis

elements.name: Date of Resolution
elements.description: Date of subsiding or termination of a symptom, problem or condition.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Condition Problems Health Concerns
US Quality Core Condition Encounter Diagnosis

elements.name: Problems
elements.description: Condition, diagnosis, or reason for seeking medical attention.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Condition Problems Health Concerns

elements.name: SDOH Problems/Health Concerns
elements.description: Social Determinants of Health-related health concerns, conditions, or diagnoses. Examples include but are not limited to homelessness and food insecurity.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Condition Problems Health Concerns

elements.name: Performance Time
elements.description: Time and/or date an activity is performed. Examples include but are not limited to vaccine or medication administration times, surgery start time, time ultrasound performed, and laboratory specimen collection time.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core DiagnosticReport Profile for Report and Note Exchange

elements.name: Procedure Reason Code
elements.description: An explanation or justification for a service requested in coded or textual form.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Procedure Not Done

elements.name: Procedure Status
elements.description: The status of planned or performed activity.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Procedure Not Done

elements.name: Procedure Type
elements.description: The specific type of medical procedure performed.

elements.name: Procedures
elements.description: Activity performed for or on a patient as part of the provision of care.
elements.notes: In QI-Core, information about principal procedure is represented using the Claim profile. In US Quality Core v1.0.0, support for the Claim profile is not required for conformance. Implementers may determine the appropriate implementation approach to support this USCDI+ Quality data element. We encourage the quality community to provide additional input on information needed to support quality measurement use cases through the HL7 ballot process for US Quality Core.

elements.name: Reason for Referral
elements.description: Explanation or justification for a referral or consultation.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Procedure Not Done

elements.name: SDOH Interventions
elements.description: Actions or services to address an identified Social Determinants of Health-related health concern, condition, or diagnosis. Examples include but are not limited to education about food pantry program and referral to non- emergency medical transportation program.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Procedure Not Done

elements.name: Author
elements.description: Actor that created or revised the data. Usage note: The actor may be a provider, a patient, a device, an outside medical record, or something else. The source of the information can be used to form assessments about its quality, reliability, trustworthiness, or can indicate where to go to determine the origins of the information.

elements.name: Author Organization
elements.description: Organization associated with author.

elements.name: Author Role
elements.description: Category of actor that participated in the creation or revision of data. Usage note: The source of the information can be used to form assessments about its quality, reliability, trustworthiness, or can indicate where to go to determine the origins of the information. Examples include but are not limited to provider, patient, family member, and device.

elements.name: Author Time Stamp
elements.description: Date and time of author action.

elements.name: Referral Status
elements.description: The current state of a referral e.g., draft, active, on-hold, revoked, completed, entered-in-error, unknown.

elements.name: Referral Date and Time
elements.description: Date and time when a provider wrote an order requesting services.

elements.name: Task Code
elements.description: Identifies the type of task that is involved.

elements.name: Task Status
elements.description: Thecurrent status of the task.

elements.name: Average Blood Pressure
elements.description: Arithmetic average of systolic and diastolic components of two of more blood pressure readings in a specified time period or according to a specified algorithm or protocol. Examples include but are not limited to 3-day morning and evening home monitoring, clinical encounter repeat average, and 24-hour ambulatory measurement.

elements.name: BMI
elements.description: Body mass index is a value derived from the mass and height of a person. The BMI is defined as the body mass divided by the square of the body height, and is expressed in units of kg/m², resulting from mass in kilograms and height in meters.

elements.name: BMI Percentile (2 - 20 years)
elements.description: BMI-for-age percentile shows how a child's weight compares to that of other children of the same age and sex.

usCore.title: US Core Pediatric BMI for Age Observation Profile

elements.name: Body Height
elements.description: The vertical measurement of an individual's stature from the feet to the top of the head when standing upright.

elements.name: Body Temperature
elements.description: Temperature is a measure of the patient's ability to generate and get rid of heat.

elements.name: Body Weight
elements.description: The measurement of weight without heavy items located on the person.

elements.name: Diastolic Blood Pressure
elements.description: Diastolic Blood Pressure

elements.name: Head Occipital-frontal Circumference Percentile (Birth - 36 Months)
elements.description: Measurement of head circumference, or OFC (occipital frontal circumference), is a reflection of head growth and is a useful tool in tracking and monitoring childhood growth and development.

usCore.title: US Core Pediatric Head Occipital Frontal Circumference Percentile Profile

elements.name: Heart Rate
elements.description: Number of times the heart beats each minute.

elements.name: Inhaled Oxygen Concentration
elements.description: Inhaled oxygen concentration is the percent of oxygen inhaled (FIO2).

elements.name: Pain Severity
elements.description: A patient's self-reported or clinically observed assessment of the intensity of pain, expressed using a standardized scale or measure.

usQualityCore.title: US Quality Core Observation Screening Assessment

elements.name: Pulse Oximetry
elements.description: Pulse oximetry is a test used to measure the oxygen level (oxygen saturation) of the blood.

elements.name: Respiratory Rate
elements.description: The respiration rate is the number of breaths a person takes per minute.

elements.name: Systolic Blood Pressure
elements.description: Systolic Blood Pressure

elements.name: Weight-for-length Percentile (Birth - 24 Months)
elements.description: The higher the percentile number, the bigger a child is compared with other kids of the same age and gender, whether it's for height or weight.

usCore.title: US Core Pediatric Weight for Height Observation Profile

notes.name: Diagnosis Rank
notes.description: Ordinal number or code that identifies the priority or significance of a diagnosis for a given encounter.
notes.notes: In QI-Core, information about principal diagnosis is represented using the Claim profile. In US Quality Core v1.0.0-ballot, support for the Claim profile is not required for conformance. We encourage the quality community to provide additional input on information needed to support quality measurement use cases through the HL7 ballot process for US Quality Core.

notes.name: Present on Admission
notes.description: An indicator of whether a patient's condition or diagnosis was known to be present at the time of admission.
notes.notes: In QI-Core, information about present on admission is represented using the Claim profile. In US Quality Core v1.0.0, support for the Claim profile is not required for conformance. We have reintroduced the Present on Admission extension for Encounter.diagnosis in this US Quality Core v1.0.0-ballot and it is (USCDI+ Quality) tagged.

notes.name: Procedures
notes.description: Activity performed for or on a patient as part of the provision of care.
notes.notes: In QI-Core, information about principal procedure is represented using the Claim profile. In US Quality Core v1.0.0, support for the Claim profile is not required for conformance. Implementers may determine the appropriate implementation approach to support this USCDI+ Quality data element. We encourage the quality community to provide additional input on information needed to support quality measurement use cases through the HL7 ballot process for US Quality Core.
